frameworks.name: resting-state functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: EEG phase-lag connectivity used as a biomarker of stimulation-induced plasticity

frameworks.name: anodal transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) plasticity
frameworks.one_line: Non-invasive cortical stimulation whose response variability the paper tries to explain

core_question: Can resting-state EEG functional connectivity predict the variable corticospinal response to anodal tDCS over M1?
key_assumptions: MEP amplitude change indexes tDCS-induced plasticity
debiased weighted phase lag index reflects volume-conduction-robust connectivity
scalp electrodes (e.g., C3) approximate underlying cortical sources
manipulations: stimulation type: anodal vs sham tDCS over left M1
key_findings: High-beta (20-30 Hz) C3-to-left-parietal connectivity strongly predicted tDCS response (cross-validated R^2 = 0.69)
Greater C3-parietal connectivity tracked stronger anodal but not sham responses
interpretations: Pre-stimulation resting connectivity is a marker of individual tDCS-induced plasticity
